Bash(python3 -c " import sys from html.parser import HTMLParser class TableParser\(HTMLParser\): def __init__\(self\): super\(\).__init__\(\) self.in_td = False self.cells = [] self.cur_cell = '' self.rows = [] self.cur_row = [] self.in_schedule = False self.found_h2 = False self.in_h2 = False self.h2_text = '' def handle_starttag\(self, tag, attrs\): if tag == 'h2': self.in_h2 = True self.h2_text = '' if tag == 'td': self.in_td = True self.cur_cell = '' if tag == 'tr' and self.in_schedule: self.cur_row = [] def handle_endtag\(self, tag\): if tag == 'h2': self.in_h2 = False if 'Schedule' in self.h2_text: self.in_schedule = True if tag == 'td': self.in_td = False self.cur_row.append\(self.cur_cell.strip\(\)\) if tag == 'tr' and self.in_schedule and len\(self.cur_row\) >= 5: self.rows.append\(self.cur_row[:]\) self.cur_row = [] def handle_data\(self, data\): if self.in_h2: self.h2_text += data if self.in_td: self.cur_cell += data p = TableParser\(\) p.feed\(sys.stdin.read\(\)\) for row in p.rows[:10]: print\(row[:5]\) ")

Bash(python3 -c " import sys, re from html.parser import HTMLParser class TableParser\(HTMLParser\): def __init__\(self\): super\(\).__init__\(\) self.in_td = False self.cur_cell = '' self.cur_row = [] self.rows = [] self.in_schedule = False self.in_h2 = False self.h2_text = '' def handle_starttag\(self, tag, attrs\): if tag == 'h2': self.in_h2 = True; self.h2_text = '' if tag == 'td': self.in_td = True; self.cur_cell = '' if tag == 'tr' and self.in_schedule: self.cur_row = [] def handle_endtag\(self, tag\): if tag == 'h2': self.in_h2 = False if 'Schedule' in self.h2_text: self.in_schedule = True if tag == 'td': self.in_td = False; self.cur_row.append\(self.cur_cell.strip\(\)\) if tag == 'tr' and self.in_schedule and len\(self.cur_row\) >= 5: self.rows.append\(self.cur_row[:]\) self.cur_row = [] def handle_data\(self, data\): if self.in_h2: self.h2_text += data if self.in_td: self.cur_cell += data p = TableParser\(\) p.feed\(sys.stdin.read\(\)\) for row in p.rows: print\('|'.join\(row[:6]\)\) ")
Bash(python3 -c "from datetime import datetime; import pytz; tz=pytz.timezone\('Europe/Budapest'\); print\('Budapest:', datetime.now\(tz\).strftime\('%H:%M'\)\)")

Bash(python3 -c " import sys from html.parser import HTMLParser class TableParser\(HTMLParser\): def __init__\(self\): super\(\).__init__\(\) self.in_td = False self.cur_cell = '' self.cur_row = [] self.rows = [] self.in_schedule = False self.in_h2 = False self.h2_text = '' def handle_starttag\(self, tag, attrs\): if tag == 'h2': self.in_h2 = True; self.h2_text = '' if tag == 'td': self.in_td = True; self.cur_cell = '' if tag == 'tr' and self.in_schedule: self.cur_row = [] def handle_endtag\(self, tag\): if tag == 'h2': self.in_h2 = False if 'Schedule' in self.h2_text: self.in_schedule = True if tag == 'td': self.in_td = False; self.cur_row.append\(self.cur_cell.strip\(\)\) if tag == 'tr' and self.in_schedule and len\(self.cur_row\) >= 5: self.rows.append\(self.cur_row[:]\) self.cur_row = [] def handle_data\(self, data\): if self.in_h2: self.h2_text += data if self.in_td: self.cur_cell += data p = TableParser\(\) p.feed\(sys.stdin.read\(\)\) for row in p.rows: print\('|'.join\(row[:6]\)\) ")
